title: Energizing Citrusy Thai Coconut Milk Curry
description: A refreshing spring-inspired Thai curry featuring the brightness of citrus and the creaminess of unsweetened coconut milk. Perfect for a meal that's both energizing and kosher-friendly.

ingredients.name: 2 cups mixed vegetables (such as bell peppers, carrots, and green beans)

instructions: Heat 2 tablespoons of coconut oil in a large pan over medium-high heat.
Add the onion and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the garlic, lemongrass, and galangal, and cook for an additional 2 minutes.
Add the grated ginger and Thai red curry paste, and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Pour in the coconut milk and stir to combine.
Bring the mixture to a simmer and cook for 10-15 minutes or until the sauce has thickened.
Stir in the fresh lime juice, sugar-free fish sauce, and salt and pepper to taste.
Add the mixed vegetables and chicken breasts (if using) and cook until the vegetables are tender, about 5-7 minutes.
Stir in the chopped cilantro and zested lime juice.
Taste and adjust the seasoning as needed.
Serve the curry over rice or noodles and garnish with additional cilantro if desired.
Store any leftovers in the refrigerator for up to 3 days and reheat as needed.
Note: If using chicken, make sure to cook it to an internal temperature of 165°F (74°C) to ensure food safety.
Note: Adjust the amount of chili peppers to your desired level of spiciness.
